meta.name: Nadeem Mohamed Soliman
meta.title: Software Engineer
meta.tagline: Building backend systems, data pipelines, and AI applications.

about.summary: Senior Computer Science and Engineering student at the German University in Cairo, graduating June 2026 with an A+ Bachelor's degree. I have shipped backend systems, data pipelines, and AI applications across banking, logistics, and enterprise software. I focus on building services that hold up under load, pipelines that don't need babysitting, and code that the next engineer can actually understand.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering and Science
education.gpaNote: Top of class — German grading scale where 1.0 is the highest possible grade

thesis.title: Vision Transformer Framework for Vehicle Intention Prediction
thesis.description: Trained a Vision Transformer (ViT) to predict vehicle maneuvers in autonomous driving scenarios, beating Uber's baseline LSTM model by 52.9%.

bullets: Built an embedded Shopify app in Remix, React, and Node.js that connects merchant stores to a logistics backend via Shopify Webhooks and GraphQL, with real-time order events across multiple accounts.
Shipped a bulk waybill generation feature that automated the full shipment creation workflow, cutting manual processing time by 80% across all merchant accounts.
Contributed to a courier management service on Bun, Hono, Clerk, MongoDB, and Next.js for real-time order tracking and courier dispatch.
Built a customer communication service on top of the Meta Cloud API for inbound and outbound messaging, conversation state across multi-step flows, and order confirmations via HMAC-verified webhooks.
Consolidated WhatsApp, SMS, and email into a single modular TypeScript backend using inheritance and polymorphism, so new channels can be added without touching existing integrations.

Built a black-box integration test suite in Vitest and Supertest that exercises the communication service purely over HTTP, asserting response shapes, MongoDB side effects, and outbound Graph API calls against a local Express mock.
Built Spring Boot microservices for a virtual banking platform with Apache Kafka for async inter-service messaging and WSO2 API Gateway for authenticated, rate-limited external routing.
Delivered REST APIs for mobile and web banking clients with OAuth 2.0 authentication, role-based access control, and request validation across all endpoints.
Integrated Keycloak SSO into OpenKM, replacing a fragmented login system and consolidating identity management inside a Dockerized MSSQL environment.
Built automated backup and recovery pipelines using Rclone for compliance and disaster recovery.
Wrote deployment and onboarding documentation that became the team's standard reference, cutting engineer ramp-up time by 60%.
Built an automated quality tracking system in VBA (Excel and Access) for the production floor, replacing a fully manual process and cutting data entry time by 70% with more accurate reporting.
Built a customer survey platform in Java, XHTML, and MySQL that let branch teams collect, store, and review structured feedback.
Trained on IBM DataPower API Gateway configuration, ACH payment processing, and enterprise banking security protocols.
Delivered a full-stack MERN banking app with user authentication, account management, and transaction history, end to end in one month.

projects.title: Vision Transformer for Autonomous Vehicle Intention Prediction
projects.description: Trained a ViT to predict vehicle maneuvers, beating Uber's LSTM baseline by 52.9% on Argoverse 2.
bullets: Beat Uber's baseline LSTM model by 52.9%.
Handled data preprocessing and temporal feature extraction on the Argoverse 2 dataset.

projects.title: Massively Scalable Food Delivery Platform (Talabat-like)
projects.description: Distributed food delivery backend across microservices for customers, restaurants, and drivers; owned the User Service end to end.
bullets: Contributed to a distributed food delivery backend across microservices for customers, restaurants, and drivers; owned the User Service end to end: JWT auth, BCrypt password hashing, and role-based access control.

Replaced shared-table SQL joins with Feign clients, published domain events over RabbitMQ, and wrote idempotent Saga consumers to update user stats on order completion and cancellation, backed by a DLQ with three-attempt retry.
Deployed the User Service on Kubernetes with health probes and a headless StatefulSet for Postgres, and added Prometheus metrics with a Grafana dashboard and structured Loki4J logs with MDC fields.

projects.title: Agentic NDA Contract Analysis System
projects.description: Agentic contract review pipeline with reasoning, policy, orchestration, and logging components.
bullets: Contributed to a 5-member team building an agentic contract review pipeline with reasoning, policy, orchestration, and logging components.
Fine-tuned a Qwen model using QLoRA on the ContractNLI benchmark to classify 17 legal hypotheses per contract as ENTAILED, CONTRADICTED, or NOT MENTIONED.
Built the vector embedding layer for semantic search over contract clauses, focusing on output reliability across real-world legal documents.

projects.title: DDoS Mitigation Tool
projects.description: Tool that detects and blocks DDoS attacks in real time.
bullets: Contributed to a tool that detects and blocks DDoS attacks in real time, working as the mitigation engineer on a four-person team.
Built the response engine that watches incoming traffic and automatically steps up from monitoring to rate limiting to blocking IPs as an attack progresses.
Integrated with Linux iptables to drop malicious traffic at the kernel level, with a scoring system to avoid blocking legitimate users.

projects.title: Athletic Tournament Management System
projects.description: Digital tournament management system for GUC students and staff.
bullets: Contributed to the planning phase of a digital tournament management system for GUC students and staff.
Delivered a Project Charter, Scope Statement, and a three-level Work Breakdown Structure with 10+ sub-activities.
Mapped stakeholder interests and set up a Jira board simulation translating WBS elements into epics, tasks, and subtasks with owners and dependencies.

projects.title: Real-Time Stock Analytics Platform
projects.description: End-to-end data pipeline with Kafka/PySpark streaming, Airflow orchestration, PostgreSQL storage, and a LangChain Text-to-SQL agent.
bullets: Contributed to a data pipeline using Airflow for orchestration and Kafka with PySpark for real-time market data streaming.
Stored processed data in PostgreSQL and helped integrate a LangChain Text-to-SQL agent for natural language querying.
Built interactive dashboards with Apache Superset to visualize streaming market data and pipeline metrics.

projects.title: Graph-RAG Travel Assistant
projects.description: Knowledge graph from 50,000+ hotel reviews with Cypher-based retrieval, named entity recognition, and a Streamlit frontend.
bullets: Contributed to a knowledge graph built from 50,000+ hotel reviews with named entity recognition and a Streamlit frontend.
Helped design a retrieval layer combining vector similarity search with Cypher-based graph traversal to ground LLM answers in structured data.
Added SHAP and LIME explainability to the ML models to make recommendations interpretable.

projects.title: All-in-One Vacation Platform
projects.description: Full-stack travel booking platform with flight/hotel search, promo codes, Stripe payments, and Amadeus live booking integration.
bullets: Contributed to a full-stack travel booking platform where users can search flights and hotels, apply promo codes, and get personalized recommendations.
Integrated Stripe for payments and Amadeus for live flight and hotel booking data.
Worked in an Agile team using Git and GitHub, contributing across the MERN stack from API design to frontend components.

projects.title: Embedded Auto Lane Detection
projects.description: Real-time lane detection system in C interfacing directly with hardware drivers on an embedded platform.
bullets: Contributed to a real-time lane detection system in C that interfaced directly with hardware drivers on an embedded platform.
Helped implement the image processing logic to identify lane boundaries and feed steering signals to the navigation layer.
Tested the system under varying lighting and road conditions to check detection reliability.

projects.title: University Academic System and Portal
projects.description: Academic management system with 50+ stored procedures, views, triggers, and functions.
bullets: Contributed to an academic management system with a normalized relational schema and 50+ stored procedures, views, triggers, and functions.
Automated registration, graduation checks, and probation rules at the database layer to enforce business logic consistently.
Helped build a role-based web portal with separate views and permissions for students, advisors, and administrators.

projects.title: Donation Ecosystem Platform
bullets: Contributed to a platform connecting donors with NGOs, taking part in requirements gathering and system design.
Helped deliver a responsive, accessible React prototype covering donor registration, campaign browsing, and NGO profiles.
Coordinated with teammates to align UI components with the agreed data model and user flows.

projects.title: The Last of Us: Legacy Game Engine
projects.description: Turn-based survival game in Java applying OOP principles and MVC architecture.
bullets: Contributed to a turn-based survival game in Java applying OOP principles and MVC architecture.
Helped build the game loop, grid-based map, and entity interaction system covering players, zombies, and collectibles.
Implemented a custom GUI and thorough exception handling to keep the game state consistent across edge cases.

certifications.title: Essential Google Cloud Infrastructure: Core Services
Getting Started with Google Kubernetes Engine

bullets: Led the technical program, coordinating a team to plan and run 36 technical sessions across the academic year.
Nearly 50 students learned from the program.
Ran weekly Python lab sessions for 20+ students covering data structures, algorithms, and OOP.
